---
title: "FactorMetric"
generated: true
description: "Factored metric $M = L L^{ top}$ with factor $L$ of shape $(d_f, k)$."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.smoothing.metric`](../nitrix-smoothing-metric.qmd)

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:FactorMetric -->
```python
class FactorMetric
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:FactorMetric -->
Factored metric $M = L L^{\top}$ with factor $L$ of shape $(d_f, k)$.

`project` computes `deltas @ L`, mapping each
$(\dots, d_f)$ feature difference to a $(\dots, k)$
projection, and the bilateral weight uses
$q = \sum (\text{deltas} \cdot L)^2 = \text{deltas}^{\top} (L L^{\top}) \,\text{deltas}$.

- $k = d_f$ -- a general anisotropic metric (cross-channel
  coupling, e.g. $L = \operatorname{chol}(M)$ from
  [`metric_from_spd`](../nitrix-smoothing-metric/metric_from_spd.qmd)).
- $k < d_f$ -- a **low-rank** metric: correlated channels are
  projected into a $k$-dimensional subspace before the norm, so
  the per-neighbour weight costs $O(k)$ rather than
  $O(d_f)$.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:FactorMetric.project -->
```python
project(deltas: Float[Array, '... d_f']) -> Float[Array, '... k']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Project feature differences through the factor $L$.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:FactorMetric.tree_flatten -->
```python
tree_flatten() -> Tuple[Tuple[Float[Array, 'd_f k']], None]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:FactorMetric.tree_unflatten -->
```python
tree_unflatten(_aux: None, children: Tuple[Any, ...]) -> 'FactorMetric'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:FactorMetric.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(factor: Float[Array, 'd_f k']) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:FactorMetric -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `d_f` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `k` | kernel or basis components |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
